title: Atomic-scale visualization of the toroidal order in a trimeric Dy(III) single-molecule toroic
authors: Michael J. Jenkins, Madalynn G. Marshall, Jie Xing, Muthu Satheeshkumar, Brandon D. Watson-Sanders, Xiaoping Wang, Christina M. Hoffmann, Gopalan Rajaraman, Huibo Cao, Rongying Jin, Zi-Ling Xue
abstract: Single-molecule toroics (SMTs) offer a unique platform for next-generation quantum devices utilizing head-to-tail spin alignments in the compounds. Presence of toroidal moments in SMTs has been essentially based on magnetometry and ab initio calculations. Here, we report observation and probe of the toroidal moment in [Dy3(OH)(teaH2)3(paa)3]Cl(OMe) [teaH3: triethanolamine; paaH: N-(2-pyridyl)-acetoacetamide] from mapping of Dy3+ magnetic susceptibility tensors by polarized neutron diffraction (PND). Neutron diffraction under variable magnetic fields demonstrates field-induced magnetization along the c-axis with toroidal moments anti-parallelly stacked, providing definite proof of the toroidal moment. Magnetometry studies confirm the toroidal ground state. For the first time, the combined use of PND, variable-field neutron diffraction, ab initio calculations, and magnetometry is introduced as a robust and quantitative methodology to probe molecular-scale toroidal magnetism. This integrated approach overcomes limitations of earlier indirect methods, establishes a benchmark framework for investigating SMTs, and provides valuable insights for the design of molecular quantum materials.
summary: The research reports on the first direct observation of toroidal magnetism in a single-molecule magnet using $	ext{Dy}_3$ complexes. By combining polarized neutron diffraction with variable-field measurements, the authors provide quantitative proof of this exotic magnetic ordering. This integrated methodology establishes a powerful new standard for investigating molecular quantum materials exhibiting complex spin correlations.
main_problem: To unambiguously observe and probe the toroidal magnetic moment at the molecular scale within Single-Molecule Toroics (SMTs).
main_result: The authors report definitive experimental proof of the toroidal moment using a combined methodology, showing anti-parallelly stacked toroidal moments via variable-field neutron diffraction.
method: The study integrates Polarized Neutron Diffraction (PND), variable-field neutron diffraction, magnetometry ($\mu$-SQUID), and *ab initio* calculations to map local magnetic susceptibility tensors.
model_or_system: The system is a trimeric Dy(III) single-molecule complex, $[	ext{Dy}_3(	ext{OH})(	ext{teaH}_2)_3(	ext{paa})_3]	ext{Cl}(	ext{OMe})$, which utilizes the large spin of $	ext{Dy}^{3+}$ ions to host toroidal magnetic ordering.
key_observables: Toroidal moment, local magnetic susceptibility tensors ($\chi$), field-induced magnetization patterns, and ground state magnetic moments ($\sim 8.77 \mu_B$).
important_parameters: Low temperatures (e.g., $5 	ext{ K}$), specific crystal structures derived from neutron diffraction, and energy gaps ($\Delta E = 1.6 	ext{ cm}^{-1}$).
main_assumptions: The combined PND/variable-field approach is necessary to overcome the limitations of earlier indirect magnetometry methods.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate toroidal moment generation (current loops), magnetic susceptibility data ($\chi$ vs $T$), isothermal magnetization ($M$ vs $H$) along different axes, and field-induced magnetic order parameters from neutron diffraction.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by first establishing the theoretical basis for toroidal moments, then presenting structural confirmations via SCND/SCXRD, followed by detailed measurements of magnetic anisotropy using PND and variable-field diffraction, culminating in a comprehensive model that links structure to the observed toroidal order.
why_interesting: This work provides a highly advanced experimental blueprint for characterizing exotic magnetic orders (toroidal magnetism) in molecular systems, which is crucial for designing next-generation quantum materials where spin chirality plays a role.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
